speaker: BELLE
text: LITTLE TOWN, IT'S A QUIET VILLAGE

speaker: A MOTHER
text: Bonjour..

speaker: A BUTCHER
text: Bonjour...

speaker: A MILKMAID
text: Bonjour...

speaker: A PRISONER
text: Bonjour...

speaker: A MERCHANT
text: Bonjour... A baker unloads a tray of rolls from his wagon to sell in the marketplace.

speaker: BELLE
text: THERE GOES THE BAKER WITH HIS TRAY,

speaker: BAKER
text: Good morning, Belle.

speaker: BELLE
text: Morning, Monsieur.

speaker: BAKER
text: Where you off to?

speaker: BELLE
text: The bookshop. I just finished the most wonderful story about a beanstalk and an ogre and a... But he's not interested. He yells over his shoulder to his wife.

speaker: BAKER
text: That's nice. Marie! The Baguettes hurry up! Belle sighs. Never mind. She continues on her way. Various townspeople talk about her as she passes.

speaker: MAN IN A CART
text: BONJOUR, GOOD DAY. HOW IS YOUR FAMILY?

speaker: ELDERLY WOMAN
text: BONJOUR, GOOD DAY. HOW IS YOUR WIFE? A harried mother with three children speaks to an egg merchant.

speaker: LARGE MAN
text: ...THAT'S TOO EXPENSIVE! Belle speaks softly to herself as all of this goes on around her.

speaker: BELLE
text: Good morning. I've come to return the book I borrowed

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh, I couldn't put it down.. Have you got anything new? She gives him the book and looks around eagerly.

speaker: BELLE
text: That's all right. I'll borrow...this one.

speaker: BOOKSELLER
text: That one? But you've read it twice!

speaker: BELLE
text: Well, it's my favorite. Far-off places, daring sword fights, magic spells, a prince in disguise...

speaker: BOOKSELLER
text: If you like it all that much... ...it's yours..
parenthetical: (chuckling and giving her the book) | (a beat)

speaker: BELLE
text: But sir!

speaker: BOOKSELLER
text: I insist.

speaker: BELLE
text: Well, thank you. Thank you very much! She gives him a beautiful smile and the tinkling bell ushers her out.

speaker: BELLE
text: OH...ISN'T THIS AMAZING? The cat gathers the kittens around for storytime.

speaker: LEFOU
text: You didn't miss a shot, Gaston. You're the greatest hunter in the whole world!

speaker: GASTON
text: I know. Belle comes around a corner on the opposite side of the square, head buried in the book. Gaston stops dead. He watches her with a proprietary glint in his eye. Lefou dumps the booty into a pile.

speaker: LEFOU
text: No beast alive stands a chance against you! A group of group of twittering local girls come out of a shop and spot Gaston. They giggle and whisper...trying to get his attention. Gaston ignores them...eyes still glued on Belle. LEFOU And no girl for that matter.

speaker: GASTON
text: It's true, Lefou. And I've got my sights set on that one. girls. He sighs enviously. Gaston Gaston points toward Belle, but Lefou's still looking at the local girls. He sighs enviously. Gaston jabs him with an elbow and points impatiently to Belle. Lefou stumbles all over himself with surprise.

speaker: LEFOU
text: The inventor's daughter?

speaker: GASTON
text: She's the one. The lucky girl I'm going to marry.

speaker: LEFOU
text: But, she's...

speaker: GASTON
text: The most beautiful girl in town.

speaker: LEFOU
text: I know, but...

speaker: GASTON
text: That makes her the best. He grabs Lefou in one beefy hand and pulls him up nose-to-nose. GASTON And don't I deserve the best?

speaker: LEFOU
text: Well, of course. I mean, please. You deserve.. Well, who else? I mean... He drops Lefou and sings.

speaker: SILLY GIRLS
text: LOOK THERE HE GOES! ISN'T HE DREAMY? MONSIEUR GASTON! OH, HE'S SO CUTE! BE STILL MY HEART! I'M HARDLY BREATHING! HE'S SUCH A TALL, DARK, STRONG AND

speaker: HANDSOME BRUTE.
text: While the silly girls sing, Gaston position himself in the spot where Belle will pass. He motions for Lefou to hurry up. Lefou arrives and dumps the booty just in time for Gaston put one foot up, hand on his hip in victorious hunter stance. Belle crosses the street and briefly glances Gaston's way...he puffs up...but she continues on without a second look. Gaston frowns, thinks for a minute, then grabs a pair of antlers from the pile and takes off after her. Belle backtracks through the marketplace...still without looking up from her book. Gaston trails her. Lefou trails him.

speaker: BLACKSMITH
text: BONJOUR!

speaker: MATRONLY WOMAN
text: YOU CALL THIS BACON?

speaker: FRUIT SHOPPER
text: WHAT LOVELY GRAPES! Another woman orders from the cheese seller.

speaker: FABRIC BUYER
text: ...TEN YARDS!

speaker: CHEESE BUYER
text: ...ONE POUND.

speaker: GASTON
text: 'SCUSE ME!

speaker: GASTON
text: PLEASE LET ME THROUGH! A woman takes a big bite of a long bread roll. She makes a face.

speaker: BAKER'S CUSTOMER
text: ...IT'S STALE!

speaker: FISHMONGER'S CUSTOMER
text: ...THEY SMELL!

speaker: BAKER AND FISHMONGER
text: MADAME'S MISTAKEN!

speaker: BOTH CUSTOMERS
text: WELL, MAYBE SO! GOOD MORNING! Belle emerges out the other side of the marketplace.

speaker: TOWNSFOLK & MERCHANTS
text: OH! GOOD MORNING! She takes off up the rise for home. Gaston emerges from the marketplace behind her. He proudly announces.

speaker: TOWNSFOLK
text: BONJOUR, GOOD DAY BONJOUR, BONJOUR, BONJOUR! Lefou hurries to catch up with both of them.

speaker: GASTON
text: Hurry up, Lefou! Belle approaches her cottage. Gaston picks up the pace of his cocky stride and leans over her shoulder.

speaker: GASTON
text: Hello...Belle. She glances up briefly.

speaker: BELLE
text: Hello, Gaston. She keeps walking. He frowns at her non-existent response and rushes to follow her.

speaker: GASTON
text: What's that you're reading? He reaches over from behind and takes the book out of her hands. Belle sighs and turns around. GASTON Ah, yes. I read this one...couldn't put it down... terrible tragedy... wept real tears. Belle hides a smile. Sure you read it. Lefou finally catches up, huffing and puffing, as Gaston tosses the book over his shoulder. It bonks Lefou on the head and falls to the ground. Belle makes a move to retrieve it...but Gaston reaches around, grabs a pair of antlers from Lefou's pile and...

speaker: GASTON
text: Belle, I brought you a present.

speaker: BELLE
text: Antlers! How thoughtful! Thank you, but I...I couldn't.

speaker: GASTON
text: Aw sure you could!

speaker: BELLE
text: It's really very kind of you, Gaston, but... ...we've no place to put it! Belle gives him the antlers, picks up the book and turns to go.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you anyway! Lefou is offended on Gaston's behalf. He strikes back.

speaker: LEFOU
text: She'd have more room if she threw out her crazy father's stupid inventions! They laugh heartily.

speaker: BELLE
text: Don't you talk about my father that way!

speaker: GASTON
text: Yeah! Don't you talk about her father that way!

speaker: BELLE
text: My father is not crazy! He's a genius. BOOM! A window in Belle's cottage blows out...colorful smoke billows out. BELLE Papa!

speaker: GASTON
text: He's done it again! C'mon! She takes off for the cottage. Drawn by the explosion, Gaston and the townspeople follow behind.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Oh my heavens...I think that's done it...after all this time... we might really have something here! The crowd arrives. They've seen this before. They shake their heads and snicker behind their hands.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa, are you all right?

speaker: MAURICE
text: All right? Why, I'm stupendous! I'm tremendous! I'm...

speaker: TOWNSPERSON
text: ...a crazy ol' fool! They snicker, but Maurice is too excited to notice. He drags her toward the workshop door.

speaker: MAURICE
text: I can't wait for you to see this.

speaker: TOWNSPERSON'S WIFE
text: Those inventions of his never work.

speaker: TOWNSPERSON'S FRIEND
text: He's out of his mind. Always has been. The words hit Belle like cruel blows. Maurice rushes inside. Belle glances back and speaks with quiet conviction.

speaker: BELLE
text: They'll see. She goes in. Excitement over, the crowd dissolves, shaking their heads and snickering. Gaston and Lefou wander off with the others.

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh, Papa! It works! You did it! You really did it I always knew you were a genius!
parenthetical: (coughing from the smoke) | (throwing her arms around him)

speaker: MAURICE
text: Well, I don't know if I'd qualify as a... "Cuckoo!" The Cuckoo Clock waves a little flag: "Faire Today!" BELLE If you're going to enter this in the Faire you'd better hurry!

speaker: MAURICE
text: The Faire! Oh my heavens, I almost forgot. I have to pack this all up and... BELLE I'll get Philippe! She runs out as he scurries around madly taking the invention apart and packing it up.

speaker: BELLE
text: Philippe! Bring the wagon! A nose reaches over the half-door, clicks the latch open and PHILIPPE, the faithful family horse, trots out.

speaker: BELLE
text: Have you got everything? You'll win this year. I just know it! He touches her cheek briefly, marveling at her belief in him.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Well if I do...we'll leave all this behind.

speaker: BELLE
text: ...and never look back Maurice clucks his tongue. Philippe whinnies and they take off. Maurice waves his hat in farewell.

speaker: BELLE
text: Keep him safe! She raises up on her tiptoes to watch them as long as she can.

speaker: BELLE
text: Good-bye, Papa! Good luck!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Goodbye Belle! Take care while I'm gone! Once they're out of sight, she slumps with exhaustion and blows a dangling wisp of hair out of her eyes.

speaker: MAURICE
text: We should be there by now. Maybe we missed the turn. Further on. MAURICE Now we'll never make it to the Faire. Belle will be so disappointed. They come to the crossroads. Philippe turns automatically, but Maurice jerks the reins. MAURICE No, no Philippe, this is the right road. Philippe whinnies and shakes his head. MAURICE I'm sure now... come along! He jerks the reins. Philippe snorts. All right, but we'll get lost! And he reluctantly turns down the road that leads into the wild forested region. They enter a dense, gloomy, ominous forest, the trees withered and tangled. Philippe doesn't like the looks of this. He whinnies nervously. The forest grows thicker, more dense, blotting out the remaining light. MAURICE This can't be right. Where have you taken us, Philippe? A cold wind whistles through. The trees creak and rustle...bare branches seem to reach for them like long, brittle fingers. MAURICE We'd better turn around. Shadows creep along the ground. Knotholes in the wizened trees stare sightlessly. A few wisps of mist swirl ominously around Philippe's hooves. Water drips from a tree branch. Something rustles through the thick, crackling underbrush. Philippe skitters nervously. MAURICE Whoa. Whoa, boy. Up ahead, a shroud of mist hangs down from the trees making them looking like weird ghostly apparitions. They press on and the mist envelops them. Philippe's ears twitch at every sound. In the distance, they hear the bone-chilling howl of a wolf! Philippe whinnies fearfully...he skitters...Maurice struggles to hold him. MAURICE Steady... Philippe, steady. There's another howl! Philippe panics. MAURICE Whoa! Philippe! Maurice loses his grip and tumbles to the ground. The wolves howl again closer now! Panicked and terrified, Philippe thunders off...with the sound of the wolves on his tail.Silence descends. Maurice climbs to his feet. The mist drops down around him like a cloak. MAURICE Philippe? Maurice stares through the mist. Two cold yellow eyes stare back. MAURICE WOLF! He stumbles backwards and turns...running blindly through the thick mist. There's a snarl then the sound of padded steps and steady panting on his trail. Maurice can hear the wolf tracking him. He catches a glimpse of something dark pacing him through the trees. Another one! MAURICE No...no... He runs headlong...stumbles over a knotted root...falls...struggles to his feet and keeps running. He looks back over his shoulder. The wolves are only grey shapes in the mist. But he can hear them...slavering hungrily...and he can see their eyes burning with hunger. Maurice looks up. He sees a light that seems to float...way off in distance. MAURICE What's that? Help! Can you hear me? Help! He scrabbles through the underbrush, fighting his way toward the light. But the trees are thick and gnarled...impossible to get through. Sharp branches tear at his clothes...almost as if they're trying to keep him away. He glances over his shoulder...yellow eyes at his heels! He turns back and almost runs into a tall ornate ironwork gate, tarnished and overgrown with age and disuse. It creaks open. Maurice leaps through and heaves it closed...just as the wolves leap for his throat! They throw themselves violently at the bars...snapping and growling, desperate to get at him. Maurice backs away...trembling. The wolves finally give up. They howl in defeat and slink away.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Well, now you've done it! I told you not to let him in!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: We could not leave him for the wolves!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right! And it's so much safer in here!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Well we don't have to tell him, do we?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Hello? His voice echoes through the vast, vacant corridors. He steps inside. The halls are dim. There's a heavy musty stillness in the air. Maurice looks around and notices a silver candelabra and a fancy wooden mantleclock sitting on the carpet in front of the door.. MAURICE Hello?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: You are lucky, Monsieur. We saw you just in time.

speaker: MAURICE
text: If you hadn't opened the gate, I don't know what I... Wait...who said that?
parenthetical: (to the unseen speaker) | (he whips around, confused)

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Monsieur Lumiere, at your service. Candelabra bows graciously but Maurice doesn't notice. He reaches down and picks up Candelabra for more light. He looks around for the mysterious speaker. I'd like to thank you for saving my life.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Yes, fine! But could you do it quietly please? Maurice whips around again.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Where are you? He feels something tugging at his cloak.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right here! He whirls but doesn't glance down. Sees nothing.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Where?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: DOWN HERE! Maurice looks down. MANTLECLOCK is staring up at him with exasperation, hands on its "hips." Mantleclock is a manic, uptight, officious English major domo...much like Basil Fawlty. His pendulum swings at a rapid pace, like his heart-rate. Someone wound him up way too tight, and he's never wound down. MANTLECLOCK And up there. He points up to the CANDELABRA in his hand. Maurice glances sideways. Two bulging eyes peer out at him. His hand covers Candelabra's mouth. He makes muffled, choking sounds. Maurice releases his hand. Candelabra gasps for air as he falls to the floor.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: AAHHH!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Incredible! How is this accomplished? Are they puppets?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Who is he talking to? Maurice grabs Mantleclock and turns him upside down. Mantleclock is mortified to be treated in this unseemly manner.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Put me down at once! Maurice pulls at his legs, his arms, his little moustache. MANTLECLOCK Stop that! Stop it I say! Maurice stops pulling and stares at Mantleclock with surprise and wonder.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Why...why you're not a puppet at all! You're...alive!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: But of course!

speaker: MAURICE
text: But...but... how can that be? It's impossible! It... it...defies the laws of all science!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: This is your last warning, sir. If you don't put me down at once I will be forced to give you a sound thrashing! Maurice quickly replaces him on the floor and looks around the castle with a renewed sense of wonder.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: For shame, Cogsworth. Is that any way to treat our guest?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Guest! Now he's a guest! While they argue, Maurice wanders over to table and discovers a music box. He pokes it to see if it's alive too. Music Box giggles in reply.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Will you play me a tune, little fella?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Poor fellow must be famished from his ordeal.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Oh no...I know what you're thinking! Now none of that! You know what the Master will do if...

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: The sound causes Mantleclock to whirl around in a panic. He runs at them arms waving.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Stop that! Stop it I say! Do you hear me? I said...QUIET!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Monsieur, as you see, we are servants with no one to serve...

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right! And we're very glad we could save your life... now let's be on our way. He pushes Maurice toward the door. Candelabra ducks around to the other side.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: But before you go...perhaps you would do us a great favor...by allowing us to serve you dinner.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Why, I'd be honored. This should be something to see.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: No! I won't allow it!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Calm yourself, Cogsworth. We'll keep it simple... ...a small repast...a morsel really.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: No! Absolutely not! Candelabra sweeps his arm toward the dining room doors which swing wide. Maurice follows him in with Mantleclock getting dragged along by the cape.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: No...no...no...no.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Esteemed Monsieur, it is with deepest pride and greatest pleasure that we welcome you tonight. And now we invite you to relax, pull up a chair, as the Dining Room proudly presents... ...your dinner!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: BE OUR GUEST! BE OUR GUEST!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: IT'S A GUEST, IT'S A GUEST SAKES ALIVE, WELL I'LL BE BLESSED!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Wh...who is it? Who are you?

speaker: BEAST
text: What are you doing here?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Master...allow me to explain. The gentleman was set upon by wolves...it was a matter of life and death...So we...invited him in for a...

speaker: BEAST
text: YOU WHAT? The force of his bellow blows all of his candles out. Mantleclock draws the curtain aside just enough for his head to stick out.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: M..Master. M.. Master. I'd like to take this moment to say...I was against it from the start.

speaker: BEAST
text: ENOUGH! The bellow blows the curtain away exposing Mantleclock. He throws his hands up over his head and waits for the end...shivering with fear. Maurice recovers from his initial shock sufficiently to come to their defense.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Please don't be angry with them. They saved my life! Beast whirls back toward Maurice and advances on him menacingly.

speaker: BEAST
text: How dare you trespass here! This is my castle... my food... my fire... Although he's grown in size and years, we can see that he remains selfish and self-centered. Terrified, the Music Box tries to bury himself deeper in the bag. It shifts with the movement and tumbles onto the floor spilling the contents. Music Box freezes. If I'm very very still maybe he won't notice me.

speaker: BEAST
text: I know what to do with thieves!

speaker: BELLE
text: There. She goes back to her book which lies open on the table. BELLE Now where was I... She picks up a fancy ribbon and continues to decorate the house as she reads. BELLE

speaker: LEFOU
text: All right. Everybody quiet! I'd like to introduce...well, you already know him... and you already love him... our very own Gaston! Gaston steps out. He's all dressed up too, looking suspiciously like a groom.

speaker: GASTON
text: I'd like to thank you all for coming to my wedding. He "reviews the troops"...admiring an enormous wedding cake. Nearby, the silly girls sob. He bows to the Vicar. GASTON But before we begin... ...I'd better go in there and propose to the girl! You know what to do, Lefou? When Belle and I come out that door...
parenthetical: (a hearty chuckle) | (a finger in Lefou's face) | (pointing to Belle's door)

speaker: LEFOU
text: I know, I know...I strike up the band! He waves his baton to demonstrate. The cronies think this is their cue and start playing the Wedding March.

speaker: GASTON
text: Not yet! He bonks Lefou on the head. Gaston gives everyone the "high" sign and confidently knocks on Belle's door.

speaker: BELLE
text: Gaston...what a "pleasant" surprise. Gaston shoves it open the rest of the way and barges inside.

speaker: GASTON
text: You know, Belle, there's not a girl in town who wouldn't love to be in your shoes. This is the day... He spots himself in the mirror and stops for a moment to check himself out. Looking good. GASTON This is the day your dreams come true... Belle's eyes go wide. Oh my God, he's going to propose. She backd up as he advances on her. GASTON ...the day you throw out these stupid books... He snatches the book out of her hand and tosses it out the window.

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh no! He stares at her, utterly shocked by he reaction. She eases out of his grasp.

speaker: BELLE
text: I mean... She falls back against the door, stunned. BELLE I'm...I'm... ... speechless!
parenthetical: (quickly so as not to embarrass him) | (searching for words)

speaker: GASTON
text: And very flattered, of course. BELLE But this comes as such a surprise that I... I don't know what to say.
parenthetical: (trying to find the way to let him down)

speaker: GASTON
text: Say...you'll marry me!

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm really sorry Gaston...but... I just don't deserve you! Her fingers find the doorknob and she pulls it open causing Gaston to tumble out the door. BELLE Thanks for asking!

speaker: LEFOU
text: She turned ya down, huh? Gaston rises up, covered in mud and smoldering. The cronies stare at him with disbelief. He grabs the baton out of Lefou's hand, breaks it over his knee and shoves a tuba over Lefou's head. He strides over to the wedding cake, grabs the porcelain bride-and-groom off the top and crushed them in his beefy hand. Gaston's enormous ego won't allow him to accept rejection. He puffs up, eyebrow arched with fierce determination and makes a public vow.

speaker: GASTON
text: I'll have Belle for my wife! Make no mistake about that! The townspeople slink away as Gaston storms off down the road. Lefou follows...still stuck in the tuba.

speaker: BELLE
text: Is he gone? The chickens cluck to indicate that he is. Belle steps out and scatters feed for the chickens. BELLE Can you imagine? He asked me to marry him. Me, the wife of that boorish, brainless.. MADAME GASTON! CAN'T YOU JUST SEE IT? MADAME GASTON! HIS LITTLE WIFE, NO SIR! NOT ME! I GUARANTEE IT!

speaker: BELLE
text: Which way, Philippe? The gnarled trunks seem like distorted faces glowering down at her. Something crackles through the underbrush nearby. The wind howls...sending rustling shivers through the trees and tangled branches. Philippe presses on through the mist. The mist clears slightly and she catches a glimpse of something up ahead. BELLE What is this place? The gate creaks as she pushes it open and starts to step through. But Philippe snorts and skitters nervously as if he can sense something unnatural about this place. She tugs on the reins. BELLE Philippe! Please. Steady...Steady. He settles down and allows her to pull him through the gate, eyes shifting nervously. Belle glances down...to see her father's hat lying on the ground. She reaches down to pick it up. BELLE Papa! Come on Philippe. They make their way across the stone bridge, Philippe's hooves clatter as he skitters nervously. She ties him to a post at the entrance to the castle. He snorts and paws at the ground, not happy about any of this. Belle goes up the steps to the castle. The door's still ajar. She calls to the residents.

speaker: BELLE
text: Hello? Is anyone here? Hello? Only silence. She steps inside. Philippe waits, eyes shifting nervously at every sound.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa? Her voice echoes back in answer. Undaunted by the ominous mystery of the place, she hurries down the corridor...her footsteps too loud in the silence...and hurries up an ornate curved staircase.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa! Are you here? It's Belle.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Couldn't be satisfied with a nice quiet dinner, could you? Had to put on airs, make a spectacle of yourself!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Perhaps we overdid it a bit.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: I'm surprised you didn't put on a parade! At that moment, Belle runs past.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa? They gape at her...frozen with surprise.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: A girl! A beautiful girl! After all these years, she has come to set us free! I am so...happy! He falls onto Mantleclock shoulders sobbing emotionally. Mantleclock pushes him off.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Nonsense! She's here because of that fellow in the dungeon. He must be her father.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Then we must help her to find him, no?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Yes! WHOOSH, they're gone. MANTLECLOCK I mean...no! I mean...wait a minute!

speaker: CHIP
text: Mama! There's a girl in the castle!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Yes, dear.

speaker: CHIP
text: A real pretty girl.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Bless my soul, wouldn't that be lovely?

speaker: CHIP
text: Really, Mama. I saw her!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Now Chip, I'll not have you making up such wild stories...getting everyone's hopes up for no good reason. Now no more foolishness. It's time to get washed up.

speaker: CHIP
text: But mom..

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Not another word! Into the tub! A twittering Feather-duster comes running in the door.

speaker: FEATHERDUSTER
text: A girl! I saw a girl in the castle!

speaker: CHIP
text: See? I told you!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Is it true? Is there a girl? Have you seen her?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Yes, yes. But keep a lid on it, won't you? We don't want the whole castle in an uproar! Candelabra calls to them from down the hall.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Cogsworth! Mrs Potts! Down here! Toute suite! They hurry to catch up with him.

speaker: FOOTSTOOL
text: Arf! Arf!

speaker: BELLE
text: Is someone there? Mantleclock and Footstool peer around the corner, Footstool wags his tail and starts to go after her, but Mantleclock drags him back.

speaker: BELLE
text: Wait! No answer. Intrigued, Belle follows the light It continues up the stairs. BELLE I'm looking for my father. I... She arrives at the top of the stairs and looks around for the person with the light. Candelabra is perched in an alcove at eye level.

speaker: BELLE
text: That's funny...I'm sure there was someone... Candelabra leans around her shoulder for a better look. He moves his eyebrows up and down: "Oooh La La!" BELLE Is anyone here? From behind the iron door, she hears faint voice below.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Belle? She recognized his voice.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa? Where are you? She whirls around. looking at the long row of doors. Which one? Maurice's face appears at the tiny slot in the bottom of the door at the end of the corridor.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Here. He thrusts his frail hand through the slot. Belle clings to it.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa!

speaker: MAURICE
text: How did you find me?

speaker: BELLE
text: Your poor hands! They're like ice!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Belle. I want you to leave this place.

speaker: BELLE
text: Who's done this to you?

speaker: MAURICE
text: No time to explain! You must go! Now!

speaker: BELLE
text: I won't leave you! The shadow of something monstrous grows on the floor. Belle can feel something behind her. She turns. And there it is...looming over her! The most frightening, horrible creature she's ever seen.

speaker: BELLE
text: The Beast's eyes are red with rage.

speaker: BEAST
text: Who are you? He stops... awed by Belle's remarkable beauty. Something flickers briefly across his gaze...some long-forgotten memory.

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm sorry to have entered your home uninvited. But this man...is my father.

speaker: BEAST
text: Then your father is a thief!

speaker: BELLE
text: That's not true! The Beast roars with fury. The stone magnifies the sounds. It's deafening.

speaker: BEAST
text: SILENCE! Belle takes a moment to gather her courage. She continues cautiously.

speaker: BELLE
text: Can't you see? He's an old man. It's cold and damp. He'll never survive in there!

speaker: BEAST
text: Then he should not have trespassed here.

speaker: BELLE
text: But he'll die. Please...what can I do? I'll do anything to save his life...I... I know... Take me instead! Beast studies her with his fierce, intelligent eyes.

speaker: BEAST
text: You would take his place? She sees a glimmer of a chance and she grabs it.

speaker: BELLE
text: Yes!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Belle...no!

speaker: BEAST
text: Done!

speaker: MAURICE
text: You can't do this! Please spare my daughter...she had no part in this.

speaker: BEAST
text: Take him to the crossroads.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa. You didn't even let me say good-bye! I'll never see him again... ...and I didn't get to say good-bye. Beast shifts his head quizzically at this outpouring of emotion.

speaker: BEAST
text: I will show you to your quarters.

speaker: BELLE
text: My quarters? But I thought...

speaker: BEAST
text: Follow me. He turns on his heel and strides briskly away. Belle angrily wipes the tears away with the back of her hand.

speaker: BEAST
text: I trust you will be comfortable here. The castle is your home. You're free to go anywhere you like. Except the West Wing. He opens the door for her. She stumbles through...in shock. BEAST One more thing. You will join me for dinner. She starts to close the door, but he puts his huge hand up to stop it. BEAST That's not a request.

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh Papa! I can't bear the thought of never seeing you again! What am I going to do?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: There, there...my dear. A handkerchief is offered to Belle. She reaches up automatically to take it.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you. She dabs at her eyes...and then looks up suddenly, realizing she's not alone. BELLE Oh! A large Wardrobe gazing at her with a sympathetic smile. WARDROBE is a fancy but faded court lady-in-waiting with pretensions to the upper class.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: You poor thing! What a dreadful afternoon you've had.

speaker: BELLE
text: I can't believe this has happened to me.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: I'm so sorry, my dear. But I have just the thing to cheer you up! Come with me. She grabs Belle by the hand and deposits her behind the dressing screen. WARDROBE There's nothing like a pretty new frock to help a girl forget her troubles.

speaker: BELLE
text: That's very kind of you. But I don't want... WHOOSH! Belle's dress flies off her head. Wardrobe looks at it with distaste.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Frankly, my dear, this does nothing for your eyes. Now what color would best suit you...ah, the blue! She pulls out a gorgeous blue brocade gown and slips it over Belle's head.

speaker: BELLE
text: I don't mean to be rude but...how can you possibly be talking to me? I mean, you're a... Well, you're not a...

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Person?

speaker: BELLE
text: Exactly.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: I'm afraid I can't answer that, my dear.

speaker: BELLE
text: Why not?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Master has forbidden us to speak of such things. She pulls Belle out from behind the dressing screen. A roomful of enchanted Objects...Atomizer, Brush, Comb, Bath Sponge etc...all "ooh and aah" over her appearance. WARDROBE Isn't she stunning? What I wouldn't give for that figure! She plunks her down at the vanity.

speaker: BELLE
text: Why has he forbidden it?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: He has his reasons. Will you look at this face? I have dressed the most elegant, noble ladies in the land and believe me... ...they'd simply kill for a face like this! Now what shall we do with her hair?
parenthetical: (admiring her in the mirror) | (a gleeful snicker) | (to the Vanity Objects)

speaker: BRUSH
text: Down... she should definitely wear it down.

speaker: COMB
text: No! Up! Sweep it up!

speaker: BRUSH
text: Down!

speaker: COMB
text: Up!

speaker: BELLE
text: Is everything in this place alive?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Of course not...whatever gave you that idea? There...you look simply exquisite! And just in time for dinner.

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm afraid... I'm not going to dinner. Wardrobe holds the place where her heart would be.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: What? But you must!

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm a prisoner not a guest.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: But the Master will be so...so...disappointed.

speaker: BELLE
text: I can't help that.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Pardon me, Master...but your supper's getting cold.

speaker: BEAST
text: We will wait.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Very good, sir. All eyes turn back to the door. Tension fills the air. Twittering Featherduster appears at the door.

speaker: FEATHERDUSTER
text: She's coming! I think she's coming! Everyone visibly relaxes.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Well, it's about time! Beast straightens up in his chair and quickly swipes a hand through his mane. But it's not Belle who appears at the door. It's Candelabra. Beast frowns.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: I am sorry, Master...but the girl has refused to come downstairs. Beast growls and SLAMS his fist angrily onto the table. Objects go flying! Mantleclock looks around frantically and jumps into an open drawer. Food and Objects have been flung everywhere. Chip, the teacup, clings to the edge of the table, his little feet scrabbling in the air. He looses his "grip" and falls... ...the hard floor rises up toward him...he squeezes his eyes shut... ...but he lands in the palm of a huge clawed hand. The Beast replaces him gently on the table.

speaker: BEAST
text: Forgive me. Mantleclock peeks out from the drawer. An Elderly Serving Bowl glances over catching him in this act of cowardice. Mantleclock climbs out...slapping his hands officiously.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Just inspecting the silver. Everything seems to be in order. Candelabra hops up on the back of a chair next to Beast.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Try to understand, Master. The girl has lost her father and her freedom...all in one day.

speaker: BEAST
text: What a fascinating creature. So beautiful...and such courage.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ah...you noticed! Candelabra raises his eyebrows up and down to the other Objects. Oooh...la...la!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Master, need I remind you that your twenty-first birthday will be here soon? The Rose has most certainly begun to wilt and that means...

speaker: BEAST
text: I know what that means!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Of course, you do. Silly me! How could you forget a thing like that? Candelabra leans down over the Beast's shoulder.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Master, have you thought that perhaps this girl could be the one to break the spell?

speaker: TEAPOT
text: "You must learn to love another and earn their love in return." The Objects all lean in listening eagerly for the answer. The Beast looks up...and they immediately busy themselves. For a brief moment, the pain is gone and his eyes fill with hope.

speaker: BEAST
text: Do you think there's a chance she might...? But then, he catches a glimpse of his huge, hulking shadow in the flickering candlelight. It's the shadow of a monster. BEAST What am I saying? A girl like that could never learn to care for me.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Look at you...defeated before you've begun. Where is your daring...your sense of adventure? The Objects nod eagerly.

speaker: BEAST
text: But I feel like a fool! Teapot freezes mid-pour. The Objects start to ease away.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ah...so it seems the girl has more courage than the beast. Mantleclock winces and throws his hands over his eyes.

speaker: BEAST
text: How dare you...! The Objects make a break for it. They leap off the table into any available hiding place. Candelabra leans back. He holds his breath...But then the Beast sees the manipulation. His growl turns into a chuckle. BEAST Touche. Objects peer out hopefully from hiding places. Mantleclock peeks between his hands.

speaker: BEAST
text: Well...I can't let a girl make me look like a coward. The Objects cheer! They leap up and down hugging each other exuberantly. Even Mantleclock forgets himself. He jumps up and down...tossing dignity to the wind.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Bravo, Master! That's the spirit... He freezes suddenly and glances over. The Serving Bowl is watching him again. Mantleclock suddenly transforms his exuberant jumping into smoothing out the lace runner with his feet. MANTLECLOCK Bit of a wrinkle here...just smoothing it out... nothing to worry about.

speaker: BEAST
text: I thought I told you to come down to dinner! Candelabra groans. Mantleclock covers his face with his hands.

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm not hungry! Of course, you could always drag me down if that doesn't suit you!

speaker: BEAST
text: Maybe I will! He angrily raises his fist to beat at the door again. Candelabra, Teapot and Mantleclock leap forward to stop him.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Master, that's not be the best way to win the girl's affections.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Please... attempt to be a gentleman.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: You must be suave...smooth...dashing... ...like me! Beast glowers at them...then back at the door. With difficulty, he manages to swallow his pride and indignance enough to try again. He speaks to Belle through the door.

speaker: BEAST
text: Will you come down to dinner... ...please! The Objects smile...that's more like it!

speaker: BELLE
text: NO! Beast growls with rage. He raises his arm to bash the door to pieces. The Objects wince. But he catches himself...and with a roar of frustration, storms away.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Well, at least he didn't break the door down! Belle paces...her feelings are a confused mixture of anger, pain and loss.

speaker: BELLE
text: How did this happen to me? How did I get locked in a castle with that...that...creature? What is he anyway? Some kind of sorcerer?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: The Master? Heavens no! The Vanity Objects giggle among themselves.

speaker: BELLE
text: Well, how else do you explain a beast who walks and talks like a human?

speaker: WARDROBE
text: As I said...we're forbidden to speak of those things. With a frustrated sigh, Belle sinks down onto the window seat.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Look on the bright side, my dear. For the rest of your life you'll have everything your heart desires: ...riches, servants, jewels, beautiful clothes! What more could a girl ask for? Belle gazes out the window.

speaker: BELLE
text: Nothing much... ...just my freedom.

speaker: GASTON
text: Who does she think she is? That girl has tangled with the wrong man!

speaker: LEFOU
text: Darn right! Gaston grabs the two mugs, downs one, then the other.

speaker: GASTON
text: Dismissed! Rejected! Publically humiliated! It's more than I can bear.

speaker: LEFOU
text: More beer?

speaker: GASTON
text: What for? Nothing helps. I'm disgraced.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Who, you? Never! Gaston, you've got to pull yourself together. GOSH IT DISTURBS ME TO SEE YOU, GASTON

speaker: LEFOU
text: NO-ONE'S GOT A SWELL CLEFT IN HIS CHIN LIKE GASTON!

speaker: GASTON
text: AS A SPECIMEN, YES I'M INTIMIDATING! ALL MY, WHAT A GUY, THAT GASTON! GIVE FIVE HURRAHS! GIVE TWELVE HIP-HIPS!

speaker: LEFOU
text: GASTON IS THE BEST AND THE REST IS ALL DRIPS! Gaston's into it now. As the number builds, he shows off his muscles, his agility, his way with a

speaker: LEFOU
text: IN A WRESTLING MATCH, NOBODY BITES

speaker: GASTON
text: AS YOU SEE, I'VE GOT BICEPS TO SPARE

speaker: GASTON
text: RIGHT! AND EVERY LAST INCH OF ME'S COVERED WITH HAIR! ALL

speaker: GASTON
text: I'M ESPECIALLY GOOD AT EXPECTORATING! PTOOEY! ALL TEN POINTS FOR GASTON!

speaker: GASTON
text: WHEN I WAS A LAD I ATE FOUR DOZEN EGGS

speaker: GASTON
text: WHEN I HUNT, I SNEAK UP WITH MY QUIVER BEASTS OF THE FIELD SAY A PRAYER!

speaker: LEFOU
text: IS THAT FAIR?

speaker: GASTON
text: I DONT CARE! ALL

speaker: GASTON
text: I USE ANTLERS IN ALL OF MY DECORATING! ALL SAY IT AGAIN! WHO'S A MAN AMONG MEN?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Help! Someone help me! The tavern keeper is drying glasses. He looks up with concern.

speaker: TAVERN KEEPER
text: Maurice? Maurice stumbles through the tavern pleading with the patrons:

speaker: MAURICE
text: Please, I need your help...he's got her...locked in the dungeon...

speaker: PATRON
text: Who? Maurice grabs him and tries to pull him toward the door.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Belle...we must go...not a minute to lose...

speaker: TAVERN KEEPER
text: Whoa. Slow down, Maurice. Who's got Belle locked in a dungeon?

speaker: MAURICE
text: A Beast...a horrible monstrous beast! They stare at him with disbelief for a moment, and then burst into laughter. The tavern keeper returns to his glasses.

speaker: TAVERN KEEPER
text: You had us worried there for a minute.

speaker: MAURICE
text: You must believe me! One of Gaston's cronies leans over Maurice.

speaker: CRONIE
text: Is it a big beast?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Huge! The cronie laughs and shoves Maurice toward another cronie. CRONIE #2 ...with an long ugly snout?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Hideously ugly! CRONIE #3 ...a sharp cruel fangs?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Yes! Yes! Will you help me? They shove him into another cronie. He glances over at Gaston who jerks his head toward the door. CRONIE #4 All right, old man. We'll help you out.

speaker: MAURICE
text: You will? Two cronies lift Maurice up by his arms, carry him to the door... MAURICE Oh, thank... ...and toss him out, SLAM! They yuck it up over their little joke.

speaker: TAVERN KEEPER
text: Crazy ol' Maurice.

speaker: PATRON
text: He's always good for a laugh. Gaston's eyes grow narrow. He must be thinking.

speaker: GASTON
text: Crazy ol' Maurice... Crazy ol' Maurice. Hmm.

speaker: LEFOU
text: YES?

speaker: GASTON
text: THEN WE...

speaker: LEFOU
text: NO! WOULD SHE...

speaker: GASTON
text: GUESS!

speaker: LEFOU
text: NOW I GET IT!

speaker: GASTON
text: YES I'M ENDLESSLY, WILDLY RESOURCEFUL

speaker: MAURICE
text: Please...I need your help...it's my daughter... she's been captured by a beast...

speaker: PEDESTRIAN
text: What nonsense!! You must believe me!

speaker: PEDESTRIAN
text: Go home, you old fool! Maurice runs up to a closed shop window and bangs on it loudly. The shopkeeper waves him away.

speaker: SHOPKEEPER
text: We're closed!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Listen to me...there's a beast...in an enchanted castle...has my Belle! He shakes his head and slams the shutters in Maurice's face. He turns with a last desperate appeal. MAURICE My daughter's life is in danger! Will no one help me? In answer, more doors and shutters SLAM shut. On the frantic desperation in Maurice's eyes we:

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Come on boy...

speaker: TEAPOT
text: That's right. Footstool slips...the tray teeters.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Careful...I said careful! Footstool finally makes it to the top without spilling the tray.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Such a good boy, you are! Footstool yaps happily and "slurps" Candelabra in the face causing the tray to slide off his cushion. Mantleclock only barely manages to catch it in time. With a glare at Candelabra, he pushes the tray back up and they continue down the hall coaxing Footstool along toward Belle's door.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: The poor child must be starved by now. They put the tray on the floor in front of her door. Mantleclock knocks on the door briskly... but instead of a little knock... it's a large resounding BANG BANG BANG! Mantleclock looks at his little fist...how'd I do that? He looks up...to find the Beast looming over him at the door. He's giving it a second try.

speaker: BEAST
text: Are you coming down to breakfast?

speaker: BELLE
text: No! Thank you.

speaker: BEAST
text: You can't stay in there forever!

speaker: BELLE
text: Yes, I can!

speaker: BEAST
text: Fine! Then go ahead and starve! He starts to turn away but notices the tray. Ah...bait. He leans back to the door. BEAST There's tray out here if you're hungry. There's a beat as she decides what to do.

speaker: BELLE
text: Move away from the door. He doesn't budge. BELLE Move away from the door! He backs up two steps. BELLE Further! He moves way back. BELLE All the way down the hall! With a disgruntled grumble, the Beast moves all the way down the hall. The door creaks open...Belle's hand shoots out...grabs the tray and whisks it back in. SLAM! The Beast stands at the end of the hall, feeling embarrassed and stupid. He glances at the Objects.

speaker: BEAST
text: I told you I'd feel like a fool.

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: He hops off the table and tags along behind. Belle proceeds down the stairs, but she hears a tinkling sound behind her. She listens for a moment, then shakes her head and continues on...past Footstool who's sleeping at the foot of the stairs. Music Box attempts to tiptoe past, but he doesn't make it. Footstool is instantly on his feet padding along behind. Down the hall, Candelabra and Featherduster are having a secret late-night tryst.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ah...my little fluff...the light of my life!

speaker: FEATHERDUSTER
text: I've been burned by you before! She pushes him away and flits off just as Belle passes by...followed by Music Box and Footstool.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: What is this? He hops over to Mantleclock who's sleeping in an overstuffed chair. CANDELABRA Cogsworth, wake up! The girl has emerged! Mantleclock jerks awake.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Well, it's about time! They take off after the others. Belle knows perfectly well she's being followed because they're making enough noise: the patter of tiny wooden footsteps, the tinkling...the thump thump of Candelabra's hopping. She whirls around suddenly. WHOOSH! They duck away. She studies the empty corridor...smiles to herself and continues on. The Objects reappear and continue to follow her.

speaker: STOVE
text: I'll thank you to keep your nose out of my pots! Surprised, Belle drops the lid. CLANG!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Shhh! You'll wake the children. Belle turns to see Teapot perched like a mother hen surrounded by her brood of sleeping cup children.

speaker: BELLE
text: Sorry.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: For heavens sake...stop your grousing and give the poor girl something to eat!

speaker: STOVE
text: There's only leftovers. But they'll have to be warmed up and my fire's gone out! Belle sees a croissant in a serving dish. She picks it up.

speaker: BELLE
text: This will be fine. Thank you. I hope you don't mind my asking...but have you always been a teapot? Mantleclock suddenly bursts in the door.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Don't answer that!

speaker: BELLE
text: I thought I heard someone following me. The rest of you might as well come out too. Candelabra, Footstool and Music Box sidle in. Candelabra bows graciously.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Welcome, mademoiselle. We are sorry about your circumstances...but very happy to have such a lovely guest.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you. This is my first time in an enchanted castle and I'm...

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Enchanted! Who said anything about the castle being enchanted? It was you, wasn't it? You're the one who spilled the beans! Stove SLAMS his door angrily and huffs and puffs indignantly.

speaker: BELLE
text: I figured it out on my own. But why is the castle deserted? Where have all the people gone? The Objects look at each other wistfully. Mantleclock wags his finger at them.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Not a word! The Master has expressly forbidden us to speak of such things

speaker: BELLE
text: I know. So I'll have to find the answers myself! Thanks for the croissant! And she takes off again.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Well! Isn't she the inquisitive one?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: She'd better not go near the West Wing! The Objects look at each other. You bet she would! WHOOSH! They're gone.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: As you were. SQUEAK! The heads all swivel back to their original positions.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: We must stop her!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: I wouldn't do that if I were you. She gasps and whirls around to find the Objects behind her. They're clearly nervous to be in this close proximity to the forbidden West Wing.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Come away from there.

speaker: BELLE
text: Why?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: It is forbidden!

speaker: BELLE
text: The Beast said I was free to go anywhere.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Except the West Wing! Belle turns to gaze back up into the gloomy stairs.

speaker: BELLE
text: So that's where I'll find the secret to all this mystery. She gathers her courage...and starts to climb the stairs. The Objects gasp!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Wait! Stop! We can't go up there!

speaker: BELLE
text: Maybe you can't...but I can! And she keeps going without a backwards glance. The Objects watch with helpless horror. Candelabra throws up his hands.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Sacre Bleu!

speaker: BELLE
text: A rose? She approaches the Rose with wide-eyed wonder. Its sparkling delicacy seems very much out of place in this filthy room. Belle grows closer. The Rose is drooping. Two more petals have fallen to the bottom of the jar. There's a creak outside the window. But Belle is entranced by the Rose and she doesn't hear it. Fascinated, she reaches out toward the jar... ...as a huge clawed hand grips the sill of the open window. Belle carefully lifts the jar... ...as the Beast climbs up over the edge. He stops...shocked at the sight of her there. Belle gazes with awe at the shimmering Rose. She reaches out... The Beast's eyes fill with fear. Her fingers are extended out to touch one soft velvet petal...

speaker: BEAST
text: NO! Belle drops the bell jar. CRASH! The Beast throws himself over the window to check on the safety of the Rose. BEAST What are you doing? Did you touch it?

speaker: BELLE
text: No... As he sees that the Rose is undamaged, his fear begins to abate and fury rises up in its place. He glowers at her...eyes burning with rage.

speaker: BEAST
text: I thought I told you never to come here! He advances on her...snarling with fury. She backs away from him fearfully.

speaker: BELLE
text: I just wanted...to find out...

speaker: BEAST
text: It's none of your business!

speaker: BELLE
text: Please...stop. But he keeps coming...a huge, monstrous, snarling, enraged creature. He swipes at a table which stands in his way. It crashes into the wall.

speaker: BEAST
text: YOU HAD NO RIGHT! NO RIGHT! Terrified, Belle turns and flees for her life. He howls again... lost in his rage.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: What is it? What are you doing? Belle furiously searches for her cloak.

speaker: BELLE
text: Looking for my cloak!

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Please my dear, don't do anything rash! There are so many things about the Master that you do not understand. She finds the cloak and pulls it out.

speaker: BELLE
text: I've seen enough to know it's too dangerous for me to stay here another minute! She throws the cloak over her shoulder and runs from the room.

speaker: BELLE
text: Philippe? She hears a joyful whinny from a stall. She rushes to him and fling her arms around his neck. He nuzzles her happily. BELLE We're getting out of here.

speaker: BELLE
text: Fly, Philippe! He whinnies and they thunder away into the night with Belle's cloak flapping behind. As they gallop through the forest. the thick mist drops around them. Belle can hardly see a few feet ahead. She reins Philippe in. BELLE Slow up. They cautiously pick their way through the dark misty woods A sharp, dry hanging branch snags Belle's cloak. She tugs at it...trying to pull free. Philippe's nostril flare. He smells something close...too close. He snorts and shakes the reins. BELLE What is it? She stares into the floating misty blackness. Then she sees the eyes...cold, menacing yellow eyes. The mist clears to reveal three hungry slavering wolves standing on a rise, gazing at them silently. Belle gasps with horror. BELLE Run! Run! Philippe breaks into a frenzied gallop. The wolves howl treacherously and leap after them. Belle is heedless to the branches that rip and tear at her as she clings to Philippe's neck. He races desperately to save their lives, mouth flecked with foam, eyes wild with panic. Belle glances back...they're right behind! A wolf leaps forward to nip at Philippe's heels. He whinnies with pain and terror. Another wolf leaps at his flanks with vicious snapping jaws. Philippe stumbles and almost goes down. He struggles to his feet and keeps running. The wolves leap for them again. Belle screams. Philippe rears suddenly throwing her to the ground. He turns...placing himself between the wolves and Belle. The wolves growl and snap... he flails at them with sharp hooves. Belle looks around desperately for some kind of weapon. She grabs a long sharp branch and runs at the wolves. BELLE Get away! The wolves turn...easier prey. They advance snarling...Belle backs up...prodding at them with the branch as they stalk her hungrily. BELLE Back! Stay back! Belle stumbles over a root...and goes down. They have her now! The wolf is at her throat! Suddenly the wolf is torn off of her from behind. It's the Beast! Belle watches...frozen with shock and horror as he grapples with the snarling, snapping wolves. They swarm on him. The creatures growl, rip, tear, and howl in a flurry of flying claws, fur and fangs. Belle pulls herself to her feet and runs to Philippe's side. But the wolves are no match for the powerful Beast. With a ferocious howl, he flings them away from him. A wolf slams into a tree and lies still. Three others see it's a losing battle. They whine piteously and make for the safety of the trees. But suddenly, the largest wolf looms up from behind the Beast! Belle gasps! BELLE Look out! The wolf lands on the Beast's back and sinks its fangs into his shoulder. The Beast howls in agony! He reaches up and tears the wolf away. He holds it in his powerful hands...it scrabbles piteously to escape. The Beast's eyes go red with savage rage. Belle gasps with horror at what he's about to do! CRACK! We see the reflection of the beast in Belle's eyes as he breaks the wolf's neck. Beast drops the animal in the snow. Belle's hand fly to her mouth...overcome with shock and revulsion. The Beast glances back at Belle...surprised to see her horrified expression.

speaker: BEAST
text: Are you all right?

speaker: BELLE
text: You didn't have to... He looks at her without comprehension and follows her eyes to the limp wolf lying in the snow. It takes him a moment to understand.

speaker: BEAST
text: His life was mine. I took it.

speaker: BELLE
text: But there's something called...mercy. The Beast grimaces with pain. He staggers forward. and falls to his knees. BELLE You're wounded! Belle looks around. what can she do to help him? She quickly unties Philippe and leads him toward the Beast. Philippe snorts fearfully. BELLE It's all right. She leads Philippe to the Beast. He pulls himself up and leans against the horse as they limp back through the snow toward the castle.

speaker: BELLE
text: This may hurt a little. Beast doesn't like the sound of that. He abruptly gets up to leave. BELLE Sit down! He sinks back down obediently. She reaches toward him with the rag. BELLE Now hold still. He cringes back as she very gently dabs at the wound. The Beast winces. He bursts out with a great...

speaker: BEAST
text: WHOOSH! The Objects vanish. Belle hesitates... fighting back her fear. She can't let him get the best of her. She gathers herself and gives him her best "stern nurse" glare.

speaker: BELLE
text: I barely touched it!

speaker: BEAST
text: If you hadn't run away this wouldn't have happened!

speaker: BELLE
text: If you hadn't frightened me I wouldn't have run away!

speaker: BEAST
text: Well, you shouldn't have been in the West Wing!

speaker: BELLE
text: And you should learn to control your temper!

speaker: BEAST
text: I can't help it!

speaker: BELLE
text: How do you know if you don't even try? He doesn't have an answer for that. They glare at each other in a silent battle of wills. But the close proximity grows uncomfortable and they both drop their eyes. Belle turns to squeeze the rag out. She glances back. He's holding his shoulder...grimacing against the pain. Belle's eyes soften. BELLE By the way... She turns back to dab gently at his arm. BELLE I forgot to thank you for saving my life. This time, he doesn't pull away. As she removes her scarf to bind the wound...he smiles secretly to himself.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Bless my soul...Dare I say it? I think they're starting to like each other.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Hmph. She's merely dressed his wound, Mrs. Potts. Nothing to get steamed up about.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Yes, but the way she dressed it. Kind and gentle and the look in her eyes...

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ah oui...my friends, the day we have waited for may be at hand!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: If only that were true, Lumiere.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ah, human again.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Human again.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Yes. Think what that means!

speaker: WARDROBE
text: AH CHERIE, WON'T IT ALL BE TOP DRAWER?

speaker: OBJECTS
text: TICK TOCK, THE TIME GOES THE DAYS PASS, THE COCK CROWS

speaker: OBJECTS
text: TICK TOCK, THE TIME FLIES A FULL MOON, A SUNRISE

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Throw those musty old curtains away, child! We've no use for them. What do you think of the floral print? Lovely! The room begins to gleam. The old curtains are pulled from the windows. Light streams in.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Sacre bleu! Mrs. Potts, you are a genius.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Go on.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: But will the Master approve of all this?

speaker: TEAPOT
text: He wants to be human again, doesn't he? Then he can't live like a beast! *ALL SWEEP UP THE YEARS, THE SADNESS AND TEARS AND THROW THEM AWAY! The musical cleaning continues. Objects change the sheets, sweep the mess, shake the carpet, scrub the walls. It's almost a dance, and then it is a dance, with animated cleaning supplies waltzing around. Perhaps Featherduster very carefully dusts the new bell jar covering the Rose. Suddenly, Beast looms in the door! The Objects hold their collective breath. Will he be angry at their intrusion? Beast moves to the wall, reaches up...and straightens a tilted painting. He flashes a grin of approval and exits. The Objects let out a collective sigh of relief.

speaker: BELLE
text: "...when Guinevere heard that Arthur was slain, she stole away to a convent. And no one could ever make her smile again." The End. Candelabra throws himself onto Mantleclock sobbing dramatically.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Ooohhhooo! That was beautiful! MANTLECLOCK Oh, see here. That's entirely enough of that! Now -- back to your duties! C'mon back to work! As the Objects leave, we see that in the shadows behind the door, the Beast has been watching and listening to the story. He steps shyly out of the shadows and approaches Belle.

speaker: BEAST
text: Belle...d'you think you could...teach...me?

speaker: BELLE
text: To do what?

speaker: BEAST
text: Er...that.

speaker: BELLE
text: To read? You never learned?

speaker: BEAST
text: Just a little and long ago.

speaker: BELLE
text: Well, at least we have something to start with, then. Come here, sit by me. Belle opens a book and gestures for Beast to sit beside her. M.O.S., the lessons begin. Outside, the moonlight gives way to dawn. Offscreen voices continue to sing. A "time-passes" transition.

speaker: OBJECTS
text: COMING TOGETHER, CLOSER AND CLOSER ...MOVING TOGETHER ...GETTING CLOSER

speaker: BEAST
text: ...ITITIT...

speaker: BELLE
text: That's right.

speaker: BEAST
text: ...ITITIT... I can't!

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh, yes, You almost had it!

speaker: BEAST
text: Rrrrrran. ...ran up the...

speaker: BELLE
text: Very good!

speaker: BEAST
text: Jack and Jill went up the hill To fetch a pail of... He gets stuck...struggling to say the word. Belle urges him on silently. With great difficulty, he finally manages to put the sounds together and finally the whole concept clicks in his head. BEAST WATER! Belle throws her hands up victoriously.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: REALLY, THAT'D BE STRANGE

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: CAN I HELP IT IF I'M T-T-TENSE?

speaker: OBJECTS
text: WHEN I'M HUMAN AGAIN! The Outdoor Objects dance as they begin to clean up the grounds. A primitive Hedge Clipper clips the hedges. A Bucket dumps water on the terrace...a Mop mops it up. A Rake meticulously rakes the fallen leaves into a pile. What used to be overgrown and spooky turns friendly and inviting:

speaker: OBJECTS
text: WE'LL BE DANCING AGAIN! WE'LL BE TWIRLING AGAIN!

speaker: WARDROBE
text: My...my...have a good romp did we? Belle laughs and nods her head. WARDROBE If I didn't know better, I'd think you might actually be having a good time here with us.

speaker: BELLE
text: I guess, I am. You know I think everything might be all right if only...
parenthetical: (realizing it herself for the first time) | (she sinks into the window seat)

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Yes...if only... Belle gazes pensively out the window.

speaker: BELLE
text: If only I could see my father again...even just for a moment.

speaker: BEAST
text: The father. Show me Belle's father.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Oh, Belle...I can't find the castle. He pounds his fists on the mantle with helpless frustration. MAURICE I've searched and I've searched! He grips the mantle...overcome with a fit of coughing.

speaker: GASTON
text: There it is.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Yep, there it is. They continue on, but slower now.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Too bad. Nobody's home. He turns to go but Gaston grabs him by the collar and plunks him back down in front of the door.

speaker: GASTON
text: What are you...scared?

speaker: LEFOU
text: Me? Naw. CREEEAAAAK! The door swings open. It's dark and spooky in there.WHOOSH! Gaston looks down to find Lefou is now hiding behind him. They hear the clink of heavy keys on a chain and suddenly a tall lean man in black with a long face and hollow eyes looms in the door.

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: May I help you? Lefou's eyes go wide. Gaston's fake bravado melts away and on his nervous smile we:

speaker: MAURICE
text: I searched this whole area...but maybe there's something I missed. A coughing fit seizes him. He loses his balance and stumbles backwards...his foot slips off the side of the steep bluff. He falls...but manages to grab a low-hanging branch. He struggles desperately to pull himself back up...but he doesn't have the strength...his frail fingers slip off and he tumbles down the steep bluff... all the way to the bottom where his fall is broken by the icy waters of a rushing stream. Maurice moans. He tries to lift himself but falls back into the water. He tries again...and barely manages to get to his knees. Bruised, soaked and filthy, he grabs onto a boulder and with a great effort, he pulls himself to his feet. He staggers forward a few steps, but his knees buckle and he falls. He lays there for a few seconds, gasping for air...and as he pulls himself up again we:

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Just where do you two think you're going? We're using the crystal...only the best tonight! Two snooty CRYSTAL CHAMPAGNE FLUTES saunter past with their noses in the air. The glass WINE GLASSES turn around and trudge reluctantly back to the cabinet. Mantleclock and Candelabra poke their heads in the door.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: How are you doing, Mrs.Potts?

speaker: TEAPOT
text: A bit flustered, I must say. I've put on many an elegant dinner...but never one as important as this.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: There is nothing to worry about.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: No, no nothing at all... just our entire future!

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Out! Both of you! I'm nervous enough as it is.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you. She sits down and he pushes her back in gently. She smiles up at him. Music Box jumps up into Belle's lap. Beast sits at one end of the table. They sip their soup. Beast handles his spoon like a pro. He glances at Candelabra who gives him a wink of approval. Mantleclock glances at the door.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Where's the music? There's supposed to be music. Hearing this, Music Box jumps up onto the table.

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: He saunters forth to save the day. Mantleclock rolls his eyes. As Music Box begins his simple little song...Mantleclock sneaks out the door leading into the ballroom.

speaker: BELLE
text: You are going to learn to dance.

speaker: BEAST
text: I...can't.

speaker: BELLE
text: Of course, you can. She holds her hand out to him. The Objects look on with baited breath. Will he have the nerve to take it? Beast hesitates...he reaches out...and their fingers touch for the first time. The Objects are thrilled! They can barely contain their excitement. BELLE Just listen to the music. One...two...three. One...two...three.

speaker: BEAST
text: One...two...three... one... They attempt a few steps. Beast crunches down on her foot

speaker: BELLE
text: Ouch!

speaker: BEAST
text: I'm sorry. Are you all right?

speaker: BELLE
text: Didn't hurt a bit.

speaker: BEAST
text: You see...I can't.

speaker: BELLE
text: Try again. All right? One...two...three. One...two...three. They attempt a few more steps.

speaker: BEAST
text: One...two...three... BELLE That's better! One...two...three. One...two...three! He smiles, getting the hang of it now. The Objects look on with joyful anticipation. Belle and the Beast twirl around as the music swells up and sweeps them away.

speaker: BEAST
text: Belle... She lifts her eyes to meet his. The feeling surges up in him and he finds the courage to say the words. BEAST

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: He is clearly in great distress.

speaker: BELLE
text: What is it? What's wrong?

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: Music Box pulls on her skirt trying to move her toward the door. Belle looks at the Beast with confusion and alarm. MUSIC BOX

speaker: BELLE
text: I'll be right back. She sweeps Music Box up in her arms. Beast lifts a hand to stop her...but she's gone. The Objects are utterly crestfallen...torn between sympathy for their Master and their own terrible disappointment.

speaker: MUSIC BOX
text: She ducks into the Beast's room...

speaker: BELLE
text: What's so... And then she sees it. The Mirror flickers with enchanted light and inside, Belle sees the image of her father. BELLE Papa? She moves toward the Mirror and picks it up. IN THE MIRROR - BELLE'S COTTAGE - ON MAURICE - HER POV He hasn't moved from where he fell near the door. It's hard to tell if he's even breathing.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Belle...

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa! Papa!

speaker: BEAST
text: He can't hear you. At the sound of his voice, she whirls around with pain and accusation in her eyes.

speaker: BELLE
text: You had this all along! You could have let me see him!

speaker: BEAST
text: You're right. I should have shown you. I'm... sorry.

speaker: BELLE
text: He may be dying! And he's all alone! Beast struggles with himself. He glances over at the Rose. A single leaf stands between him and utter doom. He looks back at Belle... and his face reflects the pain he sees in her eyes. He frowns trying to shake off the feeling. But it's no use. He's heart has gone out to her and it's breaking along with Belle's.

speaker: BEAST
text: Then you must go to him. She can't believe her ears. Neither can Candelabra, Teapot and Mantleclock who have gathered in the door behind them.

speaker: BELLE
text: What did you say?

speaker: BEAST
text: I release you. You're no longer my prisoner. She stares at him with equal parts disbelief and joy.

speaker: BELLE
text: You mean...I'm free?

speaker: BEAST
text: One of my servants will guide you through the mist.

speaker: BELLE
text: Oh thank you! Hold on, Papa. I'm coming home! She tries to return the Mirror to the Beast...but he shakes his head.

speaker: BEAST
text: Take it with you. So you'll always have a way to look back...and remember me.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you for understanding how much he needs me. He seems about to say something...that he needs her just as much. But the words won't come. She reaches out to touch his huge hand briefly...then she turns and runs from the room. Mantleclock is so overwrought he can't hold himself back.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: She was your last chance! How could you let her go?

speaker: BEAST
text: How could I keep her? He brushes past them...certain he's doomed to remain a beast for all time. Teapot shakes her head with a wistful smile.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: After all this time...he's finally learned to love.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: That's it then! That should break the spell!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: But it is not enough. She has to love him in return. They watch the Beast trudge slowly up the stairs to the castle turret.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: And now it's too late.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa! Can you hear me? He's deathly ill...shivering and feverish.. His eyes open weakly.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Can't...find...the castle.

speaker: BELLE
text: Shhh. It's all right. I'm home now. She puts his frail arm around her shoulder and helps him into bed.

speaker: MAURICE
text: I'm not a thief...run Belle!..it should have been me...me.

speaker: BELLE
text: You little rascal. She shakes her head good-naturedly and puts him down on the table. But the open flap has revealed the Mirror. She picks it up. BELLE Oh...I forgot to say good-bye. She carefully places the Mirror in a place of honor on the mantelpiece.

speaker: MAURICE
text: You really have come home. Belle whirls around. Maurice is awake. He holds out his arms and she runs to him. They cling to each other. The words tumble out.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa...I was so worried.

speaker: MAURICE
text: I thought...I'd never see you again.

speaker: BELLE
text: I missed you so much.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Are you all right?

speaker: BELLE
text: I'm fine! The Beast isn't nearly as bad as he looks. He was very kind to me. He even saved my life.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Oh! Oh my! Well, I... I guess I wasn't seeing thing after all!

speaker: BELLE
text: You should have seen it, Papa. There were hundreds of books! And I helped the Beast learn to read and... There's a sharp RAP at the door. Music Box jumps. Belle looks at her father curiously. He shrugs. She pulls the curtain aside to look out the window.

speaker: MAURICE
text: What is it?

speaker: BELLE
text: I don't know. Another loud RAP.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Open up! Belle goes to the door.

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: I'll take over from here.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Oh, sorry Monsieur d'Arque sir... I didn't mean to get in your way or bother you or anything like that. Gaston's hand appears from behind d'Arque and whisks Lefou out of the way.

speaker: BELLE
text: May I help you?

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: I've come to collect your father.

speaker: BELLE
text: My father?

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: Don't worry...mademoiselle. We'll take good care of him. Belle looks at the wagon. From this angle she can see "Maison des Loons" written on the side.

speaker: BELLE
text: Maison des Loons? My father's not crazy! D'Arque steps to the side to reveal Gaston who's leaning against the tree with a big smug grin.

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: This fellow here claims that he was ranting and raving about a beast who lives in a castle.

speaker: GASTON
text: We all heard him... ...didn't we? An affirmative murmur ripples through the crowd. The tavern owner steps forward.

speaker: TAVERN OWNER
text: I'm afraid it's true, Belle.

speaker: GASTON
text: He's a danger to himself and the village! He'll have to be locked up. Monsieur d'Arque motions to his men who move toward the door. Belle throws her arms wide...blocking their entrance.

speaker: BELLE
text: No!

speaker: GASTON
text: I'll deal with the girl. Now Belle, be reasonable. He jerks her roughly away from the door and out of the earshot of the crowd. BELLE What do you think you're doing,Gaston!

speaker: GASTON
text: Well, someone has to protect the village. The streets aren't safe with that lunatic father of yours running around loose!

speaker: BELLE
text: He's not a lunatic! She tries to get past. He grabs her arm and yanks her back, slamming her into the wall...hard! He looms over her.

speaker: GASTON
text: Of course, if you would have married me...I might have been more understanding of your poor father's condition.

speaker: BELLE
text: What are you talking about?

speaker: GASTON
text: And if you were to change your mind... I might be able to convince that unpleasant fellow to go away. The pieces fall into place. Belle stares at Gaston with shock and disbelief.

speaker: BELLE
text: What are you saying? This is blackmail!

speaker: GASTON
text: Call it what you like.

speaker: BELLE
text: You think you can force me to care for you?

speaker: GASTON
text: I'm not asking you to care.

speaker: BELLE
text: I could never marry anyone as vile as you! Never!

speaker: GASTON
text: We'll see about that! He whirls around and storms into the cottage.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Gaston? He jerks him out of bed and drags him to the door.

speaker: GASTON
text: Tell us again, old man...how big was that beast?

speaker: MAURICE
text: Well, he was... enormous! I'd say...at least, eight... no...more like ten...ten feet tall!

speaker: LEFOU
text: You don't get much crazier than that! The people shake their heads.

speaker: VILLAGER
text: Poor ol' Maurice...finally gone over the edge. Gaston drags Maurice down the stairs and shoves him at d'Arque's men.

speaker: GASTON
text: Get him out of here!

speaker: BELLE
text: But there is a beast! The crowd turns...what? BELLE And I can prove it! Belle races up the stairs and into the cottage.

speaker: BELLE
text: Show me the Beast! The Mirror shimmers....

speaker: BELLE
text: You see! My father's perfectly sane! Someone screams! Monsieur d'Arque blanches at the sight of a real beast. His men drop Maurice. They all start backing up toward the wagon.

speaker: MONSIEUR D'ARQUE
text: I'm afraid...there's been some...misunderstanding. They leap inside and takes off in one big hurry. Gaston looks from the Mirror to Belle... confused and furious that his well-laid plan has been foiled. The townspeople are very nervous.

speaker: NEIGHBOR
text: So it's true!

speaker: NEIGHBOR'S WIFE
text: There is a beast!

speaker: MAURICE
text: Didn't I tell you?

speaker: LADY
text: What a horrible creature!

speaker: LADY'S FRIEND
text: Will he hurt us?

speaker: BELLE
text: He wouldn't dream of it! There's nothing to fear. Gaston watches this exchange and it occurs to him that she's defending the Beast.

speaker: A WIFE
text: We're not safe with a savage beast in the forest!

speaker: BELLE
text: Please, don't be afraid. He's not dangerous.

speaker: GASTON
text: Not dangerous? I ought to know a vicious animal when I see one!

speaker: BELLE
text: He's not an animal!

speaker: GASTON
text: Oh, really? Take a look at the size of that monster's fangs! Belle advances on Gaston with fire in her eye.

speaker: BELLE
text: Yes, the Beast looks frightful, but he's never bullied me... ...he's never threatened me and he's never tried to blackmail me! He's not a monster, Gaston! You are! Gaston's face twists with rage. He raises a hand to strike her..! But the crowd gasps...stopping him. His gaze falls on the Beast in the Mirror and he finds an outlet for his revenge. Gaston grabs the Mirror out of Belle's hand. He holds it over his head, inciting the crowd to panic.

speaker: GASTON
text: The Beast will make off with your children! Several mothers gasp and grab their little ones close. GASTON He'll come after them in the night!

speaker: BELLE
text: No!

speaker: GASTON
text: No one will be safe until his head is mounted on my wall I say...we kill the Beast! The crowd cheers!

speaker: BELLE
text: No! I won't let you do this! Belle tries to get the Mirror away from Gaston but he grabs her arm roughly.

speaker: GASTON
text: If you're not with us...you're against us! Bring the old man! The cronies grab Maurice. Belle and Maurice struggle as they drag them toward the cellar workshop.

speaker: BELLE
text: Get your hands off me! GASTON We can't have them running off to warn the creature. They throw them into the basement. SLAM! Belle and Maurice beat on the door.

speaker: BELLE
text: Let us out!

speaker: GASTON
text: We'll rid the village of this beast! Who's with me?

speaker: CRONIE
text: I am!

speaker: CRONIE
text: I am!

speaker: LEFOU
text: I am.

speaker: GASTON
text: Gather torches and weapons! We'll lay siege to the castle and bring back the monster's head!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: I knew it! I knew it was foolish to get our hopes up.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Perhaps it would have been better if she had never come at all.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Shush now...both of you! Belle brought a little cheer into this house and we have to be grateful for that.

speaker: FEATHERDUSTER
text: But now the spell will never be broken.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Do not fret, my sweet frou-frou. The endless years may drag on and on...but I will never leave your side!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: That's what she's worried about. Featherduster pushes Candelabra away. Footstool's ears perk up. He runs to the window yapping. They look up hopefully.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Can it be?

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Is it she? They leap up and run to the window. They look out eagerly.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Who are they?

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Invaders! They're storming the castle!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Mon Dieu! They have the Mirror!

speaker: GASTON
text: Take whatever booty you can find...but remember, the Beast is mine!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: If it's the Master they want... they'll have to come through us first! Boil the oil! Evacuate the porcelain and china! United we stand! Who's with me? He turns around to find he's alone. Everybody else is running out the door. MANTLECLOCK Wait...I haven't discussed strategy yet... Come back, I say! As head of this household...I demand that you stop right there! Omph! The door SLAMS shut. He gets up... runs to the door and beats on it. MANTLECLOCK Open this door at once! Right! Fine! But you're courting disaster without me! Oh spiffing! Absolutely spiffing!
parenthetical: (running after them) | (he trips and falls flat) | (but they're long gone) | (he turns)

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Pardon, sir.

speaker: BEAST
text: Leave me in peace.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: I wouldn't trouble you... but the castle is under attack.

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Oh dear, this is most disagreeable! However did they find us through the mist?

speaker: TEAPOT
text: They have the Mirror. Beast looks at Teapot sharply. If they have the Mirror that can only mean one thing.

speaker: BEAST
text: Then Belle must have...

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Don't you even think it! That darling girl would never do a thing like that! Suddenly, there's an incredible BOOM! BOOM! BOOM! from below.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: Oh lord! They're breaking in the doors! What shall we do, sir? But Beast is too heartbroken to care.

speaker: BEAST
text: Let them come. Belle's scarf slips out of his hand and falls to the floor.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: To the door! Toute Suite! They barricade themselves against the door. BOOM!,BOOM! But they can't hold it. CANDELABRA This is not working!

speaker: GASTON
text: Again!

speaker: GASTON
