text: Follow me. They proceed cautiously through the piles of furniture toward the staircase. The Objects jump up and follow behind silently. One of the men senses something behind. He whips around. There's no one there. But there is something different about that furniture.

speaker: CRONIE
text: That's funny. I could have sworn that table was just over there. CRONIE #2 You're seeing things. The first cronie shrugs and they continue on. But then Lefou spots an ornate silver candelabra. He approaches it with avarice in his eye.

speaker: LEFOU
text: Heeey...this oughta bring a pretty penny! He turns back to wink at the others as he grabs for Candelabra...who ducks. Lefou misses. He turns back quizzically. Candelabra doesn't move. Lefou peers in closer...and Candelabra pokes him in the eyes. LEFOU Aaah!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: Seal the gates! SLAM! The door shuts... sealing off their escape. The Objects advance menacingly and the battle begins. As the Objects rise up to defend the castle, Gaston continues on in his search for the Beast.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: ...have no idea how to prepare a proper defense... ...make an absolute mess of it and then come crying to me... He slams the last book on top of the pile and uses it like a ladder to reach the doorknob. He manages to turn the knob just as the door is shoved open from the other side... knocking Mantleclock and the pile of books to the ground. Gaston peers in...looking for the Beast. Mantleclock climbs to his feet and runs for the open door just as Gaston goes out again...slamming it in his face. Mantleclock stands for a moment...head against the door... then starts to build his pile all over

speaker: MAURICE
text: I could kick myself for leaving it out there in the yard to rust!

speaker: BELLE
text: What have I done? Maurice comes over to lay a comforting hand on her shoulder.

speaker: MAURICE
text: You mustn't blame yourself, child. Belle looks up at him with anguish in her eyes.

speaker: BELLE
text: But it's all my fault! I lead them right to him!

speaker: MAURICE
text: And saved your old Papa from terrible fate I might add.

speaker: BELLE
text: But you heard Gaston! You heard what he's going to do when he finds the Beast! I have to get out of here! I have to warn him! Music Box looks up at the high window then down at a discarded ball of twine lying on the floor and gets an idea.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Don't worry...we'll think of something. The twine flies up to the window. Music Box follows winding it around his internal cylinder. At the window, he swings out through the broken glass...

speaker: MAURICE
text: If I didn't know better I'd say that sounds exactly like my... Belle peeks through to see the Invention on a collision course with the door!

speaker: BELLE
text: Look out! She pulls him away just.as the Invention comes crashing through! Moonlight streams in through the open door as Belle helps Maurice to his feet. BELLE Are you all right?

speaker: MAURICE
text: By jiggedy, it worked! I knew I had something there! Wait a minute...if we were both locked up in here than who...? Belle hears a weak tinkle...tinkle...coming from the crash site. She digs around and picks up the Music Box who's in worse shape than ever... springs and gears sticking out all over.

speaker: BELLE
text: It was you, wasn't it? Music Box tries to "say" something but all he can do is make a tiny pathetic broken little sour note. He drops his eyes. He knows there's no hope for him. Belle looks helplessly at her father who gently takes the Music Box.

speaker: MAURICE
text: What are you looking so down in the dumps about? I'll have you fixed up in no time! Music Box perks up enough to manage a weak sideways grin. Belle looks anxiously at the open door...desperate to go to the Beast but worried about leaving her father.

speaker: BELLE
text: Papa...I...I... Maurice can see how difficult this is and he makes it easier for her.

speaker: MAURICE
text: You'd better get yourself back to that castle, young lady, before it's too late! Her face lights up with a relieved smile. She throws her arms around him.

speaker: BELLE
text: Will you be all right?

speaker: MAURICE
text: I'll be just fine...now go! She kisses Maurice and pats Music Box on the head.

speaker: BELLE
text: Thank you! And she runs out the door. Maurice immediately starts fiddling with Music Box.

speaker: MAURICE
text: Now let me see...where is this little gear supposed to go?

speaker: BELLE
text: Can you find your way back to the castle, Philippe? He whinnies eagerly. BELLE Then, fly! Philippe rears and they thunder away in a flurry of hooves.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right then! Let me at them! He arrives at the bottom brandishing a sword in each hand. MANTLECLOCK Have at you blackguards! I'll give you the thrashing of your... He trails off as he sees that the other Objects have handled the situation without him.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Oh. As the Objects send up a rousing victory cheer we:

speaker: GASTON
text: Beast! Come on out! I know you're here! Footstool comes running up...snarling and growling viciously. Gaston kicks Footstool who flies into the wall...CRASH! Footstool yips piteously. This is all the Beast can take. The door explodes open and the Beast is there...looming over Gaston...roaring with fury!

speaker: BEAST
text: GET OUT OF MY CASTLE! Gaston gasps...utterly taken aback by the sight of the real Beast. But his hunter's instincts kick in and he raises his sword.

speaker: GASTON
text: Not without your head! With a battle cry, he runs at the Beast. The Beast roars and knocks him away with powerful arm. Gaston goes at him again. At they fight, they back up onto Belle's room.

speaker: BELLE
text: Hurry, Philippe! Please, hurry! He careens around a corner and and plunges off the road into the mist shrouded forest.

speaker: BELLE
text: Beast! She leaps off her horse and dashes up the steps to the door. The crowd tries to pull her back.

speaker: TAVERN KEEPER
text: No, Belle!

speaker: BELLE
text: Let me go! She pounds on the door.

speaker: BELLE
text: Let me in! Please! At the sound of her voice, Candelabra, Teapot, Mantleclock freeze in their tracks. Can it be?

speaker: TEAPOT
text: It's Belle! Mantleclock opens the door and Belle runs in...frantic over the Beast's welfare. She flies past them and up the stairs. Candelabra falls to his "knees" dramatically.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: She has come home!

speaker: GASTON
text: Let me go! Let me go! Please...don't hurt me...I'll do anything... ...anything! But the Beast is lost in his animal fury! With a roar, he prepares to snap Gaston's neck... Below, the crowd gasps with horror at what he's about to do. The Beast roars again...caught in an internal struggle. But try as he might, the Beast can't do it. He's too human now. Finally, with a growl of frustration, he drops Gaston.

speaker: BEAST
text: Get out. Gaston holds his throat...gasping his air...his face twisted with humiliation and outrage. He sees his sword on the ground. With a treacherous cry, he grabs his sword and runs at the Beast from behind. Belle screams!

speaker: BELLE
text: Beast! The Beast turns at the sound of her voice. His face lights up briefly to see her...and Gaston stabs him in the back! With a howl of agony, the Beast swipes at Gaston with his powerful arm... knocking him off the balcony!

speaker: BELLE
text: Please forgive me...I tried to get back in time to warn you!

speaker: BEAST
text: I...know. He winces from the pain.

speaker: BELLE
text: If you hadn't let him go this wouldn't have happened! The shadow of a smile crosses his face and he quotes her words.

speaker: BEAST
text: But there's something called...mercy. At least...I got to see you...one last time.

speaker: BELLE
text: Don't talk like that! You'll be all right! I won't let you die! He glances over at the Rose. The last petal will fall any second now.

speaker: BEAST
text: It's better...this way.

speaker: BELLE
text: No! It can't be...please...please... I love you! Her tears spill down to splash on the Beast's cheek. And as she leans down to kiss him... ...the enchanted Rose bursts into full bloom! The spell is broken and magic fills the air! The Beast's eyes blink open...he glances down at his paws...as they transform into human hands! He brings his hand up close to his face and gazes on it with wonder. He reaches up to touch his face. It's smooth! Belle watches...stunned speechless.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right, fine! That snuffs it! We're doomed forever! Suddenly, he's encircled in a glimmering shroud of magic and...he's transforms in a short, rotund, mustached Major-Domo. But he's so caught up in his proclamations of doom that he doesn't notice. MANTLECLOCK I knew it would turn out like this! I knew... Behind him, Candelabra magically transforms into a tall, smooth, suave, dashing Maitre D'. He looks down at himself with disbelief! He taps the muttering Major-Domo on the shoulder. He glances up...Candelabra throws open his arms!

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: She has broken the spell! The Major-Domo gapes with shock! He glances over at Teapot who has transformed into a plump Cook who dabs at her eyes with a kerchief.

speaker: TEAPOT
text: She's set us free! Mantleclock dares to look down at himself. He gasps!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Good lord! So she has! The Maitre 'D bursts into tears of joy.

speaker: CANDELABRA
text: I am so happy! He grabs the Major-Domo by the shoulders and kisses him dramatically on both cheeks.

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Stop that! Stop it at once! Get hold of yourself, man!

speaker: WARDROBE
text: Isn't it marvelous? I'm positively svelte!

speaker: MANTLECLOCK
text: Right! Off you go! Don't worry about a thing. The Duke flicks the reins. Philippe whinnies in response and they clatter across the bridge.

speaker: MAITRE 'D
text: Bon voyage! He waves good-bye then turns to wink at the twittering Maid, She rolls her eyes. The Cook holds the hands of her children who wave good-bye while the short-legged Dog barks and gives chase.

speaker: BELLE/ THE DUKE
text: Good-bye! Good-bye! From an upper window, Maurice leans out to wave good-bye as well. They pass the gates and turn onto the road.

speaker: DUKE
text: Wait... He looks back over his shoulder... ...at the Old Wise Woman who smiles and bows graciously as they disappear around the bend.
